problem_addressed: Language model agents lack specialized interfaces for complex software engineering tasks, limiting their performance compared to human engineers using tools like IDEs.
core_claim: Custom agent-computer interfaces significantly enhance LM agents' ability to autonomously perform software engineering tasks.
key_contributions: Introduction of SWE-agent system with custom agent-computer interface (ACI)
Achieving state-of-the-art performance on SWE-bench (12.5% pass@1) and HumanEvalFix (87.7%)
Demonstrating that interface design critically impacts agent behavior and performance
novelty: Unlike prior work using generic interfaces (shell/interpreter), SWE-agent introduces a purpose-built ACI with LM-friendly commands and feedback formats specifically designed for software engineering tasks.
method_summary: SWE-agent enables LMs to interact with computers through a custom interface that translates LM actions into filesystem operations, file edits, and program executions with structured feedback.
key_innovation: The ACI's LM-friendly command structure and environment feedback format, which bridges the gap between LM reasoning and computer operations by providing structured, interpretable interactions.
assumptions: LMs can effectively follow structured command interfaces
Repository navigation and file editing are primary bottlenecks in software engineering tasks
Execution feedback is critical for iterative problem-solving
theoretical_grounding: None provided; evaluation is entirely empirical through benchmark performance.
datasets_used: SWE-bench test set (286 trajectories for GPT-4, 35 trajectories for Claude 3 Opus)
hyperparameters: Cost limit of $4.00 per task episode
headline_result: Resolution performance across different turns with varying success rates

results_table.value: Resolved instances show earlier mean with fewer trajectories >120 steps
edit, python, find_file: 71 times (Turns 2-4, Localization File)
search_dir, open, search_file: 21 times (Turns 1-3, Localization File)

ablations: Not explicitly stated in provided text
improvement_over_baseline: Claude 3 Opus shows lower resolution rate on SWE-bench Lite compared to GPT-4 on full SWE-bench
best_result: Resolution performance across different turns with varying success rates

title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
